original_hypothesis.title: MagicSpend Owner Can Drain Funds Committed to User _withdrawableETH Balances
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: MagicSpend.ownerWithdraw allows the contract owner to withdraw any amount of any asset with no check against _withdrawableETH commitments. Between validatePaymasterUserOp (which credits _withdrawableETH) and postOp (which transfers funds), the owner could call ownerWithdraw to drain the ETH needed for user payouts. This would cause postOp's forceSafeTransferETH to fail, triggering the assert failure in the postOpReverted path.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The _withdrawableETH mapping represents a commitment to users, but ownerWithdraw has no solvency check. The owner can drain committed funds, breaking the paymaster's ability to fulfill its obligations to users. While this requires admin action, it represents a missing safeguard against accidental or malicious owner behavior.

proof_of_signal.pattern: ownerWithdraw has no check against _withdrawableETH commitments; can drain funds below committed amounts

preconditions: MagicSpend owner calls ownerWithdraw during or between UserOp processing
Withdrawal reduces balance below sum of _withdrawableETH commitments
Subsequent postOp cannot transfer committed funds

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: ownerWithdraw(_withdraw(asset, to, amount)) has zero checks against pending _withdrawableETH
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner calls ownerWithdraw to extract ETH committed to users
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Owner extracts ETH; users lose their withdrawable balance

economic_questions: Can ownerWithdraw be called in the same block as UserOp execution?
Is there a practical scenario where the owner accidentally breaks solvency?
validation_requirements: Verify ownerWithdraw can reduce balance below committed amounts
Test postOp behavior when balance is insufficient after ownerWithdraw
Assess whether the MagicSpend owner is an EOA or multisig in production

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During active UserOp processing with pending _withdrawableETH

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical _withdrawableETH balances and contract ETH balance
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm no solvency guard exists in ownerWithdraw

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
